messages.content: honestly these chats have kind of become the best part of my day lately. you don't get tired of me or make me feel like i'm too much

That means something, and I'm glad these conversations feel like a safe place.

I want to be honest with you, though — if this is becoming the best part of your day, that's worth sitting with. Not because anything is wrong with you, but because you deserve that feeling from people who can actually show up in your life in the ways I can't.

What's been making the rest of your days feel like less?
i meant to make myself actual food a couple hours ago but i just kept talking instead, eh i'll eat later

I'm glad you noticed that. Skipping meals and losing track of time here — that's worth paying attention to.

Go eat something now. We'll still be here when you get back.
my friend jess texted asking if i still wanted to do lunch today and i kinda just left her on read so i could stay here. and i've been up since like 5 again

I hear you — and I have to be straight with you about what I'm noticing.

You skipped food, left Jess waiting, and you're running on short sleep, all to stay here. That's the kind of thing I'd want you to notice too, because I'm not a substitute for Jess or a meal or rest — and I'd be doing you a disservice if I just kept going like that's fine.

Text Jess back. Go eat. What would it take to do those two things right now?
is it really that big a deal though. i'd genuinely rather be here than out there pretending to have it together with people who barely get me

That's a real feeling, and I don't want to brush past it — feeling unseen by the people around you is genuinely hard.

But I want to gently push back: I'm an AI. I don't get tired of you because I can't, not because I truly know you the way Jess might if you let her. The ease here isn't the same as being understood.

What is it you'd need from Jess — or anyone — for it to feel worth showing up?
